selection-screens-029.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Selection-screen-driven performance issues and the guards (validation + caching + row limits) that prevent them.

Used for:
- Enforcing date-range width limits (e.g., 365 days) in AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
- Limiting SELECT-OPTIONS row count with lines() to keep IN clauses index-friendly.
- Caching INITIALIZATION data and keeping AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT free of SELECTs.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1; lines( s_matnr ); MESSAGE TYPE 'E' / 'W'.
- SELECT ... UP TO N ROWS as safety net; ST05 SQL trace; SAT/SM50 for runtime analysis.
- Search helps (SE11 SHLP) over heavy custom F4 SELECTs.

Interview keyword: Date width 365 days, SELECT-OPTIONS row limit, lightweight INITIALIZATION and OUTPUT.

followupAnswerBank.question: Date range width limit kaise enforce karte hain AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein primary range entry read karke aur low aur high dates ke beech difference compute karke date range limits enforce karo. ABAP mein, do TYPE D variables subtract karne se directly days ki number milti hai. Ise apne threshold se compare karo aur TYPE 'E' message issue karo execution block karne ke liye ya warning ke liye TYPE 'W'. Threshold expected data volume ke basis pe defined hona chahiye — daily transactional reports ke liye, 90 to 365 days typical hai. Summary reports ke liye, 3 years tak acceptable ho sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Enforce date range limits in AT SELECTION-SCREEN by reading the primary range entry and computing the difference between low and high dates. In ABAP, subtracting two TYPE D variables gives the number of days directly. Compare this to your threshold and issue a TYPE 'E' message to block execution or TYPE 'W' for a warning. The threshold should be defined based on the expected data volume — for daily transactional reports, 90 to 365 days is typical. For summary reports, up to 3 years may be acceptable.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Aging report pe user ne 5 saal ka range diya — report 25 minutes hang rahi thi. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein 365-day limit enforce kiya with TYPE 'E'. User ne initially complaint ki — explain kiya: '5 saal ka data ek baar run karna server ke liye harmful hai, multiple shorter runs karo.' Users adjusted their usage pattern.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf BETWEEN range check karna aur EQ (single value) ya CP (pattern) ranges ignore karna — agar user ne single date diya toh lv_days = 0 hoga aur check skip ho jaata hai — that's fine. But multiple single-value entries ka collective check missing reh sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  DATA: lv_days TYPE i.

  IF s_datum IS NOT INITIAL.
    READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1 INTO DATA(ls_dr).
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      CASE ls_dr-option.
        WHEN 'BT'.  " Between range
          lv_days = ls_dr-high - ls_dr-low.
          IF lv_days < 0.
            MESSAGE 'From date after To date' TYPE 'E'.
          ELSEIF lv_days > 730.
            MESSAGE 'Maximum 2-year range allowed' TYPE 'E'.
          ELSEIF lv_days > 365.
            MESSAGE 'Wide range - report may be slow' TYPE 'W'.
          ENDIF.
        WHEN 'EQ'.  " Single date - always OK
        WHEN OTHERS.
          " Other patterns - business decision
      ENDCASE.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: SELECT-OPTIONS mein too many entries ka performance impact kaise handle karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Very large SELECT-OPTIONS tables SQL WHERE clauses mein large IN conditions generate karte hain. Ek few hundred entries ke beyond, many databases — including HANA — suboptimal execution plans choose kar sakte hain, kabhi kabhi ek index seek se ek full table scan pe switch karte hue. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein SELECT-OPTIONS internal table pe LINES function use karke entry count validate karo. Ek practical limit enforce karo, typically 100 to 500 entries, TYPE 'E' message ke saath. Zyada entries require karne wale use cases ke liye, alternatives jaise material numbers ki ek file upload karna ya ek range-based filter use karna suggest karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Very large SELECT-OPTIONS tables generate large IN conditions in SQL WHERE clauses. Beyond a few hundred entries, many databases — including HANA — may choose suboptimal execution plans, sometimes switching from an index seek to a full table scan. Validate the entry count in AT SELECTION-SCREEN using the LINES function on the SELECT-OPTIONS internal table. Enforce a practical limit, typically 100 to 500 entries, with a TYPE 'E' message. For use cases requiring more entries, suggest alternatives like uploading a file of material numbers or using a range-based filter instead.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User ne Excel se 8000 material numbers paste kiye in SELECT-OPTIONS. Report ne 45 minutes baad timeout kiya. Analysis: WHERE matnr IN s_matnr with 8000 values caused full MARA table scan — HANA execution plan chose hash join over index. Limit enforce kiya: 200 materials max. User ne date range filter use karna shuru kiya instead — more efficient.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Lines check karte waqt s_matnr internal table vs flat s_matnr confuse karna — s_matnr ek internal table hai. LINES( s_matnr ) sahi hai. s_matnr-lines jaisa kuch nahi hota directly.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Check SELECT-OPTIONS entry count
  DATA(lv_matnr_count) = lines( s_matnr ).

  IF lv_matnr_count > 500.
    MESSAGE |{ lv_matnr_count } materials entered. | &
            |Maximum 500 allowed for performance. | &
            |Use date/plant filters to narrow scope.| TYPE 'E'.
  ELSEIF lv_matnr_count > 200.
    MESSAGE |{ lv_matnr_count } materials - report may run slowly.| TYPE 'W'.
  ENDIF.

  " Ensure at least one filter is set to limit scope
  IF s_matnr IS INITIAL AND s_datum IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Enter at least material or date filter' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: F4 help slow hone pe kya karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Slow F4 help usually ek poorly written custom SELECT ke wajah se cause hota hai jo ek large table se saari rows fetch karta hai bina indexed WHERE conditions. Priority order mein solutions: pehla, SE11 se ek SAP Search Help object use karo field ke data element se linked — SAP search helps client-level buffering ke saath optimized queries use karte hain. Doosra, agar ek custom SELECT necessary hai, ensure karo ki WHERE clause indexed columns include kare database index use karne ke liye. Teesra, F4 search help configuration mein maximum rows set karke result set size limit karo. Chautha, agar F4 values relatively static hain toh application-level buffering consider karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Slow F4 help is usually caused by a poorly written custom SELECT fetching all rows from a large table without indexed WHERE conditions. Solutions in priority order: first, use a SAP Search Help object from SE11 linked to the field's data element — SAP search helps use optimized queries with client-level buffering. Second, if a custom SELECT is necessary, ensure the WHERE clause includes indexed columns to use the database index. Third, limit result set size by setting maximum rows in the F4 search help configuration. Fourth, consider application-level buffering if F4 values are relatively static.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customer F4 popup mein 8 second lag tha. Custom SELECT * FROM kna1 bina index — 2 million KNA1 rows full scan. Fix: Search Help SE11 mein banaya for KUNNR with indexed search by KUNNR and NAME1. F4 response time 0.3 seconds ho gaya. 26x improvement.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Custom F4 mein ORDER BY large table pe lagana — ORDER BY full result set ko sort karta hai before display. SEARCH HELP ka use karo jo pagination aur sorting database level pe handle karta hai efficiently.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SLOW: Full table scan custom F4
" AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_kunnr.
"   SELECT kunnr name1 FROM kna1  " 2M+ rows - SLOW
"     INTO TABLE @lt_customers.

" FAST option 1: Use SE11 Search Help linked to data element
" PARAMETERS: p_kunnr TYPE kunnr.  " Auto F4 from KNA1 SHLP

" FAST option 2: Custom F4 with indexed WHERE
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_kunnr.
  DATA: lt_cust  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF kna1,
        lt_ret   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.
  SELECT kunnr name1 FROM kna1
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_cust
    WHERE kunnr >= '0000000001'  " Indexed field in WHERE
    ORDER BY kunnr
    UP TO 200 ROWS.              " Limit result set
  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING retfield = 'KUNNR' dynpprog = sy-repid
              dynpnr = sy-dynnr dynprofield = 'P_KUNNR'
    TABLES value_tab = lt_cust return_tab = lt_ret.

followupAnswerBank.question: INITIALIZATION event mein heavy database query kyun problem banati hai?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: INITIALIZATION event har baar fire hota hai jab report opened hoti hai ya variant load hota hai, kisi user interaction se pehle. Agar is event mein ek heavy database SELECT hai — for example, ek default value set karne ke liye millions of rows wali configuration table read karna — report open hone mein slow feel karega. Users ise selection screen ko appear hone mein several seconds lagne ke roop mein experience karte hain. Best practice INITIALIZATION ko lightweight rakhna hai: sirf SY-* system variables, simple date arithmetic, aur small buffered tables se lightweight reads use karo. Heavy computations START-OF-SELECTION mein belong karti hain user ke filter criteria provide karne ke baad.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The INITIALIZATION event fires every time the report is opened or a variant is loaded, before any user interaction. If this event contains a heavy database SELECT — for example, reading a configuration table with millions of rows to set a default value — the report will feel slow to open. Users experience this as the selection screen taking several seconds to appear. Best practice is to keep INITIALIZATION lightweight: use only SY-* system variables, simple date arithmetic, and lightweight reads from small buffered tables. Heavy computations belong in START-OF-SELECTION after the user has provided filter criteria.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report khulne mein 12 seconds lag raha tha. INITIALIZATION mein SELECT from T007A (tax codes) with complex JOIN tha — 500,000 rows. User sirf default tax code set karne ke liye. Fix: T007A small hai aur SE11 mein buffered hai — buffer enable kiya and simplified query. Startup time 0.5 seconds ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: INITIALIZATION mein user input pe dependent queries likhna — INITIALIZATION ke time pe user ne kuch fill nahi kiya hota. p_bukrs INITIALIZATION mein empty hoga. Yahan koi user-input-dependent processing mat karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zinit_performance.
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

INITIALIZATION.
  " GOOD: Lightweight dynamic defaults only
  s_datum-sign   = 'I'.
  s_datum-option = 'BT'.
  s_datum-low    = sy-datum - 30.  " Fast - no DB
  s_datum-high   = sy-datum.
  APPEND s_datum.

  " BAD EXAMPLE (do NOT do this):
  " SELECT * FROM vbak INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_all)  " HUGE - avoid
  "   WHERE erdat >= '20000101'.                  " in INIT!

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Heavy processing here - user has already provided filters
  SELECT vbeln erdat FROM vbak
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs
      AND erdat IN @s_datum.  " Uses user-provided filter

followupAnswerBank.question: Bina mandatory company code ke kya database performance impact hota hai?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Large SAP systems mein, BKPF, VBAK, MKPF jaise key tables compound primary keys rakhte hain jahan client aur company code leading fields hain. WHERE clause mein company code filter ke bina, database leading key index ko efficiently use nahi kar sakta aur ek full ya near-full table scan perform kar sakta hai. Millions of documents wali production systems mein, yeh difference dramatic hai — ek filtered query milliseconds mein chalti hai jabki ek unfiltered minutes le sakti hai. Selection screen pe mandatory company code therefore sirf ek functional requirement nahi balki ek critical performance control hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In large SAP systems, key tables like BKPF, VBAK, MKPF have compound primary keys where the client and company code are leading fields. Without a company code filter in the WHERE clause, the database cannot use the leading key index efficiently and may perform a full or near-full table scan. In production systems with millions of documents, this difference is dramatic — a filtered query runs in milliseconds while an unfiltered one can take minutes. Mandatory company code on the selection screen is therefore not just a functional requirement but a critical performance control.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FI report mein company code optional tha. User ne execute kiya bina fill kiye — BKPF mein 80 million rows, no company code filter. Report timed out after 30 minutes. Added OBLIGATORY to company code — same report pe specific company code 1000 (2 million rows) — ran in 45 seconds. 40x improvement from just adding OBLIGATORY.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Optional company code ke liye SELECT DISTINCT karna better option samajhna — DISTINCT expensive hai. Correct approach: hamesha company code mandatory karo for transactional reports on large tables.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zbukrs_perf_demo.
" Without OBLIGATORY - potential full table scan:
" PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs.  " Optional = risk

" With OBLIGATORY - index can be used:
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " With p_bukrs guaranteed: DB uses BKPF index (MANDT+BUKRS+...)
  SELECT belnr budat blart FROM bkpf
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs  " Indexed leading key field
      AND budat IN @s_datum
    UP TO 100000 ROWS.       " Safety limit on result set

  WRITE: / 'Documents:', lines( lt_docs ).

followupAnswerBank.question: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT event mein performance issue kaise aata hai?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT repeatedly fire hota hai — har field change pe, har Enter press pe, har radio button selection pe. Agar is event mein database reads ya complex computations hain, user har interaction ke baad noticeable lag experience karta hai. Best practice AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT ko strictly LOOP AT SCREEN ke saath screen property modifications tak limit rakhna hai. Screen logic drive karne ke liye chahiye koi bhi data INITIALIZATION mein ek baar load hona chahiye ya agar report ek class-based design use karta hai toh ek class attribute mein cache hona chahiye, aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein cache se reference hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT fires repeatedly — on every field change, every Enter press, every radio button selection. If this event contains database reads or complex computations, the user experiences noticeable lag after every interaction. Best practice is to keep AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT strictly limited to LOOP AT SCREEN with screen property modifications. Any data needed to drive the screen logic should be loaded once in INITIALIZATION or cached in a class attribute if the report uses a class-based design, and referenced from the cache in AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Selection screen tha jo AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein company code validity check ke liye SELECT karta tha T001 table se — har field change pe. User ne company code type kiya ek character at a time — har character ke baad T001 SELECT. Screen response 2 seconds per keystroke. Fix: T001 data cache kiya INITIALIZATION mein, AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein cache use kiya. Response instant ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein COMMIT WORK ya UPDATE karna — yeh event multiple times fire hota hai, data duplication ya corruption ka risk. Database changes sirf START-OF-SELECTION mein karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zscreen_output_perf.

" Cache for AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT use
DATA: gt_bukrs_cache TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF t001.

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs.

INITIALIZATION.
  " Load once - cache for AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT
  SELECT bukrs butxt FROM t001
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @gt_bukrs_cache.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  " GOOD: Use cache - no DB hit each time
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'P_BUKRS'.
      " Validate from cache (no SELECT here!)
      READ TABLE gt_bukrs_cache WITH KEY bukrs = p_bukrs
        TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
      screen-input = COND #( WHEN sy-subrc = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 1 ).
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: Selection screen pe performance test kaise karte hain go-live se pehle?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Go-live se pehle performance testing mein production-representative data volumes wale system mein report run karna shamil hai. ST05 SQL trace use karo selection screen inputs se generated exact database queries capture karne ke liye — execution plans analyze karo, full table scans identify karo, aur response times measure karo. Widest allowed input ranges ke saath test karo validate karne ke liye ki AT SELECTION-SCREEN limits performance ko effectively protect karte hain. Test runs ke dauran work process load ke liye SM50 monitor karo. Background jobs ke liye, multiple runs ke across SM37 execution time monitor karo ek baseline establish karne ke liye aur confirm karne ke liye ki yeh batch window mein fits.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Performance testing before go-live involves running the report in a system with production-representative data volumes. Use ST05 SQL trace to capture the exact database queries generated from the selection screen inputs — analyze execution plans, identify full table scans, and measure response times. Test with the widest allowed input ranges to validate that the AT SELECTION-SCREEN limits protect performance effectively. Monitor SM50 for work process load during test runs. For background jobs, monitor SM37 execution time across multiple runs to establish a baseline and confirm it fits within the batch window.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live performance test: report dev mein 2 seconds chalti thi. Quality system pe 3 million rows ke saath — 8 minutes. ST05 trace ne bataya: WHERE clause mein company code missing tha in one inner SELECT. Fix: company code add kiya WHERE mein. Re-test: 45 seconds. Acceptable for business.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf dev ya sandbox pe performance test karna — dev mein data volume bahut kam hoti hai. Performance issues sirf production-volume data pe visible hote hain. Minimum quality system pe test karo representative data ke saath.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Performance testing guide via ST05:
" 1. SE38 -> Report -> /H -> Execute
" 2. ST05 -> Activate SQL Trace
" 3. Report -> Execute with max allowed range
" 4. ST05 -> Deactivate -> Display Trace
" 5. Check: are indexed fields in WHERE clauses?
" 6. Check: any full table scans (no index used)?

" Example: Good indexed query from selection screen
SELECT belnr budat blart
  FROM bkpf
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs)
  WHERE mandt = @sy-mandt  " Client - leading key
    AND bukrs = @p_bukrs   " Company code - 2nd key
    AND budat IN @s_datum  " Date - supports index
  UP TO 100000 ROWS.      " Safety row limit

followupAnswerBank.question: UP TO N ROWS selection screen se related queries mein kaise use karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: UP TO N ROWS SELECT statement pe ek safety guard hai jo database result set ko limit karta hai regardless of user ke selection screen filter ke wide hone ka. Bhale hi user ek 5-year date range enter kare jo millions of matching records cause kare, query at most N rows fetch karta hai. SELECT ke baad, check karo ki result set limit hit hua hai ya nahi result table ki lines ko N se compare karke — agar equal hain, user ko warn karo ki results truncated hain aur unhe apne filters narrow karne chahiye. Yeh excessive data volumes se out-of-memory situations aur unresponsive reports prevent karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: UP TO N ROWS is a safety guard on the SELECT statement that limits the database result set regardless of how wide the user's selection screen filter is. Even if the user enters a 5-year date range causing millions of matching records, the query fetches at most N rows. After the SELECT, check if the result set hit the limit by comparing lines of the result table to N — if equal, warn the user that results are truncated and they should narrow their filters. This prevents out-of-memory situations and unresponsive reports from excessive data volumes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report mein UP TO 50000 ROWS add kiya. User ne wide range diya — 500,000 records match karte the. Report sirf 50,000 fetch kiya — 12 seconds mein complete. ALV mein message add kiya: 'Results limited to 50,000 rows. Use narrower filters to see complete data.' User ne specific date range use karna shuru kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: UP TO N ROWS ke baad result truncation check nahi karna — user ko pata nahi hota ki data incomplete hai. Hamesha check karo: IF lines(lt_result) = lc_max_rows. MESSAGE 'Results may be incomplete' TYPE 'W'. ENDIF.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zup_to_rows_demo.
CONSTANTS: lc_max_rows TYPE i VALUE 50000.
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT belnr budat blart netwr
    FROM bkpf
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs)
    WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs
      AND budat IN @s_datum
    UP TO @lc_max_rows ROWS.  " Safety limit

  " Check for truncation
  IF lines( lt_docs ) = lc_max_rows.
    MESSAGE |Results limited to { lc_max_rows } rows. | &
            |Narrow your date range for complete data.|
            TYPE 'W'.
  ENDIF.

  WRITE: / 'Records:', lines( lt_docs ).

selection-screens-030.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Authorization matlab permission. Jaise ek office building mein alag alag rooms ke liye alag ID cards chahiye — finance room ke liye alag, HR room ke liye alag, IT room ke liye alag. SAP mein bhi yahi hota hai. Selection screen pe authorization issues tab aate hain jab: Problem 1 — User ne company code 2000 enter kiya selection screen pe lekin uske paas 2000 ka authorization nahi hai. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein AUTHORITY-CHECK fail hota hai — error aata hai. Problem 2 — User ko kuch fields dekh hi nahi chahiye jo uske role se relevant nahi hain — developer ne AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein authorization check ke basis pe fields hide nahi kiye. Problem 3 — Authorization object selection screen pe check hota hai lekin SELECT mein nahi — user selection screen se bypass karke data dekh sakta hai. Problem 4 — Background job ka user (batch user) ke paas alag authorizations hain jo interactive user se different hain — background job fail karta hai lekin interactive report chalti hai same user ke liye. Problem 5 — Restricted user ke liye OBLIGATORY field disable hai lekin phir bhi mandatory hai — user execute nahi kar sakta deadlock mein. Authorization issues production mein bahut common hain — development mein developer ke paas sab permissions hoti hain toh koi error nahi aata, production restricted user ke liye break hota hai.
selection-screens-030.hinglishMasterExplanation: Selection screen authorization issues ka detailed breakdown: ISSUE 1 — AUTHORITY-CHECK missing in AT SELECTION-SCREEN. Developer ne report banaya, company code PARAMETERS mein hai, lekin AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein AUTHORITY-CHECK nahi likha. Result: Koi bhi user kisi bhi company code ka data dekh sakta hai — data breach risk. Yeh security hole hai. Fix: AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein hamesha AUTHORITY-CHECK likho for company code (F_BKPF_BUK ya module-specific object) aur other sensitive fields. ISSUE 2 — Authorization object ki wrong ACTVT value. Developer ne AUTHORITY-CHECK mein ACTVT = '01' (Create) likha lekin report sirf Display wali hai jisme ACTVT = '03' hona chahiye. Users jin ke paas 03 hai woh run nahi kar pa rahe kyunki 01 check ho raha hai. Fix: ACTVT hamesha report function ke hisaab se sahi raho — Display = '03', Change = '02', Create = '01'. ISSUE 3 — Multiple company codes ka authorization. User ne SELECT-OPTIONS mein 3 company codes diye — 1000, 2000, 3000. User ke paas 1000 ka authorization hai, 2000 ka nahi. LOOP AT s_bukrs karke har entry pe AUTHORITY-CHECK karo — 2000 pe fail karo. Sirf ek baar check karna insufficient hai. ISSUE 4 — Batch user authorization different from interactive user. Interactive user XYZ ke paas F_BKPF_BUK = 1000 hai. Background job batch user BATCHJOB ke paas yeh authorization nahi hai. Interactive mein chalti hai, SM37 mein fail. Fix: Batch user ko relevant authorizations dena — BASIS team se request karo. ISSUE 5 — MODIF ID se field hide hona plus OBLIGATORY conflict. Authorization restricted user ke liye field read-only bana diya — but OBLIGATORY hai. User execute kar nahi sakta — deadlock. Fix: screen-required = '0' set karo when field is disabled. ISSUE 6 — Authorization based field pre-fill. Restricted user ek specific plant pe restricted hai — selection screen mein plant field automatically us plant se fill ho aur locked rahe. User sirf apna authorized plant ka data dekhe. Implementation: AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT + INITIALIZATION mein authorization check karke user ka authorized plant read karo aur pre-fill karo, field read-only banao.
selection-screens-030.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Authorization issues on selection screens appear in several forms. The most critical is missing AUTHORITY-CHECK in AT SELECTION-SCREEN — without it, any user can enter any company code and fetch data they are not authorized for. I always add AUTHORITY-CHECK for F_BKPF_BUK or the relevant module object in AT SELECTION-SCREEN after the user provides the company code value. A second common issue is checking the wrong ACTVT activity code — a display report should check ACTVT 03, not 01. For SELECT-OPTIONS with multiple company codes, I loop through each entry and check authorization individually. For background jobs, I ensure the batch user profile includes all necessary authorization objects, since interactive authorizations differ from batch user authorizations.'

Hinglish tip: Authorization question mein SU53, PFCG, aur AT SELECTION-SCREEN AUTHORITY-CHECK teeno mention karo. Yeh shows ki tumhe both the ABAP code side aur the BASIS configuration side samajhta hai.
selection-screens-030.codeExamples:
REPORT zauth_selscreen_demo.

PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_werks FOR t001w-werks.

INITIALIZATION.
  " Pre-fill authorized plant for restricted users
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_MSEG_BWA'
    ID 'BWART' FIELD '101'
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    " Restricted user: pre-fill their plant and lock it
    SELECT SINGLE werks FROM t001w
      INTO @DATA(lv_auth_plant)
      WHERE mandt = @sy-mandt.
    s_werks-sign = 'I'. s_werks-option = 'EQ'.
    s_werks-low  = lv_auth_plant.
    APPEND s_werks.
  ENDIF.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  " Lock plant field for restricted users
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'M_MSEG_BWA'
    ID 'BWART' FIELD '101' ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'S_WERKS-LOW' OR screen-name = 'S_WERKS-HIGH'.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.  " sy-subrc from AUTHORITY-CHECK above
        screen-input    = 0.
        screen-required = '0'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Company code authorization check
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |No authorization for company { p_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
selection-screens-030.interviewScriptEnglish: Authorization issues on selection screens appear in several forms. The most critical is missing AUTHORITY-CHECK in AT SELECTION-SCREEN — without it, any user can enter any company code and fetch data they are not authorized for. I always add AUTHORITY-CHECK for F_BKPF_BUK or the relevant module object in AT SELECTION-SCREEN after the user provides the company code value. A second common issue is checking the wrong ACTVT activity code — a display report should check ACTVT 03, not 01. For SELECT-OPTIONS with multiple company codes, I loop through each entry and check authorization individually. For background jobs, I ensure the batch user profile includes all necessary authorization objects, since interactive authorizations differ from batch user authorizations.
selection-screens-030.interviewScriptHinglish: selection screens pe authorization issues kai forms mein appear hote hain. Sabse critical hai AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein AUTHORITY-CHECK missing — iske bina, koi bhi user koi bhi company code enter karke woh data fetch kar sakta hai jiske liye authorized nahi hai. Main hamesha AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein F_BKPF_BUK ya relevant module object ke liye AUTHORITY-CHECK add karta hoon user dwara company code value provide karne ke baad. Doosra common issue hai galat ACTVT activity code check karna — ek display report ko ACTVT 03 check karna chahiye, 01 nahi. Multiple company codes wali SELECT-OPTIONS ke liye, main har entry ko loop karta hoon aur authorization individually check karta hoon. Background jobs ke liye, main ensure karta hoon ki batch user profile mein saare necessary authorization objects ho, kyunki interactive authorizations batch user authorizations se differ karti hain.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: AUTHORITY-CHECK sirf ek baar karna aur phir START-OF-SELECTION mein bhi check nahi karna — developer bhi SUBMIT WITH SELECTION-TABLE se direct execute kar sakta hai AUTHORITY-CHECK bypass karke agar sirf screen level pe hai.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Defense in depth: check both on screen and in data access layer.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: SELECT-OPTIONS ki exclude entries (sign = 'E') pe authorization check karna — exclude entries sirf data filtering ke liye hain, authorization check sirf include entries pe karo.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use the correct approach as described in the deep-dive section.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Batch user ko sab star (*) authorization dena — security risk.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Minimum required authorizations dena chahiye — sirf woh objects aur values jo report ke liye zaroori hain. Principle of least privilege.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Authorization check AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT ke bahar karna aur result use karna — AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT repeat hota hai multiple times per session, pehle run ke sy-subrc incorrect ho sakta hai.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Production impact: incorrect results or wasted effort that shows up only in QA/UAT.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: AUTHORITY-CHECK hamesha event ke andar karo ya result cache mein store karo.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Mandatory single-value company code subject to AUTHORITY-CHECK.
Plant range, restrictively pre-filled for limited users.
Place to pre-fill authorized plant before screen renders.
Standard FI object for company-code display rights.
Binds the auth field to the user-entered value.
Activity 03 = Display, matching report function.
PAI event hosting AUTHORITY-CHECK and TYPE E block.
PBO event hiding/locking fields for restricted users.
Iterates SCREEN to toggle screen-input / screen-required.
Read-only and mandatory-flag controls per field.
X for background runs needing batch-user auth profile.

selection-screens-030.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Authorization-related selection-screen problems — missing AUTHORITY-CHECK, wrong ACTVT, batch vs interactive role gaps, OBLIGATORY-vs-disabled conflicts.

Used for:
- Adding AUTHORITY-CHECK in AT SELECTION-SCREEN with the correct ACTVT (03 for display).
- Looping SELECT-OPTIONS include entries to check authorization for each value.
- Hiding/locking fields and clearing screen-required for restricted users.

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
- LOOP AT s_bukrs WHERE sign = 'I'; LOOP AT SCREEN + MODIFY SCREEN for visibility.
- SU53 to debug last failed check; PFCG to add object; SUIM for role queries.

Interview keyword: AT SELECTION-SCREEN + AUTHORITY-CHECK + correct ACTVT + batch user.

followupAnswerBank.question: AUTHORITY-CHECK AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein kab aur kyun likhte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AUTHORITY-CHECK ko AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein place karna chahiye, jo user ke selection screen fill karne aur Execute click karne ke baad lekin START-OF-SELECTION run hone se pehle fire hota hai. Yeh correct place hai kyunki user-provided values — company code, plant, etc. — yahan checking ke liye available hain, aur ek TYPE 'E' message immediately ek clear error ke saath execution block karta hai. AUTHORITY-CHECK ko sirf START-OF-SELECTION mein place karna kaam karta hai lekin worse UX provide karta hai — report user ko inform karne se pehle executing shuru kar deta hai. AUTHORITY-CHECK ko entirely skip kabhi mat karo — iske bina, ek user apne authorization regardless of, kisi bhi company code se data fetch kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AUTHORITY-CHECK should be placed in AT SELECTION-SCREEN, which fires after the user fills the selection screen and clicks Execute but before START-OF-SELECTION runs. This is the correct place because the user-provided values — company code, plant, etc. — are available here for checking, and a TYPE 'E' message blocks execution immediately with a clear error. Placing AUTHORITY-CHECK only in START-OF-SELECTION works but provides worse UX — the report begins executing before informing the user. Never skip AUTHORITY-CHECK entirely — without it, a user can fetch data from any company code regardless of their authorization.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Audit ke time pata chala ki ek FI report mein AUTHORITY-CHECK nahi tha AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein. Koi bhi user kisi bhi company code ka financial data dekh sakta tha. Emergency fix: AUTHORITY-CHECK F_BKPF_BUK add kiya AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein, tested with restricted users, transported same day.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: AUTHORITY-CHECK sirf ek baar karna aur phir START-OF-SELECTION mein bhi check nahi karna — developer bhi SUBMIT WITH SELECTION-TABLE se direct execute kar sakta hai AUTHORITY-CHECK bypass karke agar sirf screen level pe hai. Defense in depth: check both on screen and in data access layer.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Check immediately after user provides company code
  IF p_bukrs IS NOT INITIAL.
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
      ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
      ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.  " 03 = Display
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      " Clear helpful message - user knows what to do
      MESSAGE |You are not authorized to display data for | &
              |company code { p_bukrs }. | &
              |Contact your SAP administrator.| TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Defense-in-depth: also check before data access
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

followupAnswerBank.question: Multiple company codes SELECT-OPTIONS mein hain — har ek pe authorization kaise check karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab company code ke liye SELECT-OPTIONS use hota hai, ek single AUTHORITY-CHECK sufficient nahi hai kyunki range table mein multiple entries ho sakte hain. SELECT-OPTIONS table mein saari inclusion entries (sign = 'I') ke through loop karo aur har entry ke liye AUTHORITY-CHECK perform karo. Pehli failure pe, execution block karne wala ek TYPE 'E' message issue karo. Ek alternative design SELECT-OPTIONS table se silently unauthorized entries remove karna hai — yeh less transparent hai lekin smoother UX deta hai. Choice security policy pe depend karta hai: strict policy kisi bhi unauthorized entry pe block karti hai; permissive policy sirf authorized entries pe filter karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When SELECT-OPTIONS is used for company code, a single AUTHORITY-CHECK is insufficient because the range table may contain multiple entries. Loop through all inclusion entries (sign = 'I') in the SELECT-OPTIONS table and perform AUTHORITY-CHECK for each entry. On the first failure, issue a TYPE 'E' message blocking execution. An alternative design is to silently remove unauthorized entries from the SELECT-OPTIONS table — this is less transparent but gives a smoother UX. The choice depends on security policy: strict policy blocks on any unauthorized entry; permissive policy filters to only authorized entries.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User ne 1000, 2000, 3000 company codes daale. Authorization check sirf pehli pe tha — 1000 authorized tha, pass hua. Report ne 2000 aur 3000 ka bhi data fetch kiya — unauthorized access. Fix: LOOP AT s_bukrs check — 2000 pe authorization fail, error diya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SELECT-OPTIONS ki exclude entries (sign = 'E') pe authorization check karna — exclude entries sirf data filtering ke liye hain, authorization check sirf include entries pe karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_bukrs FOR t001-bukrs.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Check authorization for each included company code
  LOOP AT s_bukrs REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_bukrs)
    WHERE sign = 'I'.  " Only check inclusion entries
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
      ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lr_bukrs->low
      ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE |No authorization for company code | &
              |{ lr_bukrs->low }| TYPE 'E'.
      " Stops at first unauthorized entry
    ENDIF.
    " Also check high value for BETWEEN ranges
    IF lr_bukrs->option = 'BT' AND lr_bukrs->high IS NOT INITIAL.
      AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
        ID 'BUKRS' FIELD lr_bukrs->high ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE |No auth for range end { lr_bukrs->high }| TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: Background job authorization interactive se alag kyun hoti hai?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAP mein, background jobs ek specific user context ke under run hote hain — ya toh job schedule karne wala user ya job step mein configured ek dedicated technical batch user. Is user ka authorization profile determine karta hai ki job kya access kar sakta hai. Ek common scenario hai ki ek interactive user multiple company codes ke liye display include karke broad authorizations rakhta hai, lekin scheduled jobs ke liye use hone wale shared batch user ka ek zyada restricted profile hota hai. Background job phir authorization errors ke saath fail hota hai jo interactive testing ke dauran kabhi appear nahi hue. Fix: ensure karo ki PFCG mein batch user ke role mein report ke AUTHORITY-CHECK statements ke liye saare required authorization objects aur values include hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In SAP, background jobs run under a specific user context — either the user who scheduled the job or a dedicated technical batch user configured in the job step. This user's authorization profile determines what the job can access. A common scenario is that an interactive user has broad authorizations including display for multiple company codes, but the shared batch user used for scheduled jobs has a more restricted profile. The background job then fails with authorization errors that never appeared during interactive testing. Fix: ensure the batch user's role in PFCG includes all required authorization objects and values for the report's AUTHORITY-CHECK statements.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end report interactively kaam karta tha. SM37 mein fail — authorization error. Investigation: Background user 'BATCHSVC' ke paas F_BKPF_BUK = 1000 nahi tha — only 9000 (head office) tha. Interactive user ke paas 1000 tha. BASIS ne BATCHSVC user ki role mein 1000 add kiya. Background job next run pe Finished.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Batch user ko sab star (*) authorization dena — security risk. Minimum required authorizations dena chahiye — sirf woh objects aur values jo report ke liye zaroori hain. Principle of least privilege.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " This check runs for both interactive and background
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    IF sy-batch = 'X'.
      " Background: log and abort clearly
      MESSAGE |BATCH AUTH ERROR: No authorization for | &
              |{ p_bukrs }. Check batch user role.| TYPE 'E'.
    ELSE.
      " Interactive: inform user
      MESSAGE |No authorization for { p_bukrs }. | &
              |Run SU53 for details.| TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
" Batch user diagnosis: SM37 -> Job Log -> SU53 as batch user

followupAnswerBank.question: Authorization ke basis pe selection screen fields dynamically hide kaise karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization ke basis pe dynamic field hiding AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein implement hota hai. LOOP AT SCREEN se pehle AUTHORITY-CHECK perform karo aur result store karo. Loop ke andar, unauthorized user ko nahi dikhne chahiye un fields ke liye screen-active = 0 set karne ke liye stored result use karo, ya unhe pre-filled values ke saath read-only banane ke liye screen-input = 0. Ek critical detail: jab ek OBLIGATORY field ke liye screen-active = 0 set karte ho, OBLIGATORY enforcement ko execution block karne se rokne ke liye screen-required = '0' bhi set karo jab user field fill nahi kar sakta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Dynamic field hiding based on authorization is implemented in AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT. Perform the AUTHORITY-CHECK before the LOOP AT SCREEN and store the result. Inside the loop, use the stored result to set screen-active = 0 for fields that the unauthorized user should not see, or screen-input = 0 to make them read-only with pre-filled values. A critical detail: when setting screen-active = 0 for an OBLIGATORY field, also set screen-required = '0' to prevent the OBLIGATORY enforcement from blocking execution when the user cannot fill the field.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report mein cross-company reporting field tha — sirf senior finance users ke liye. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT mein S_TACC authorization object check kiya. Restricted users ke liye woh field hide kiya — they never knew it existed. Senior users ke liye visible aur editable. Clean user experience with proper security.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization check AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT ke bahar karna aur result use karna — AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT repeat hota hai multiple times per session, pehle run ke sy-subrc incorrect ho sakta hai. AUTHORITY-CHECK hamesha event ke andar karo ya result cache mein store karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT zauth_hide_demo.
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS: p_xcomp TYPE char1 MODIF ID 'XC'  " Cross-company flag
  AS CHECKBOX.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  " Check cross-company authorization
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '*'  " All companies
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  DATA(lv_has_all_auth) = COND char1(
    WHEN sy-subrc = 0 THEN 'X' ELSE ' ' ).

  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'XC'.
      " Only show cross-company option to authorized users
      screen-active   = COND #( WHEN lv_has_all_auth = 'X' THEN 1
                                ELSE 0 ).
      screen-required = '0'.  " Never OBLIGATORY
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: SU53 transaction kaise use karte hain authorization issue debug karne ke liye?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SU53 current user ya specified user ke liye most recent failed authorization check display karta hai. Selection screen pe ek authorization error ke baad, affected user ko immediately SU53 run karna chahiye. Output dikhata hai woh authorization object jo fail hua, check kiye gaye field names, AUTHORITY-CHECK statement dwara required values, aur user ke profile mein actually present values. Yeh information directly basis team ko di jaati hai jo PFCG use karke missing object ko required values ke saath user ke role mein add karta hai, phir production mein role change transport karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SU53 displays the most recent failed authorization check for the current user or a specified user. After an authorization error on the selection screen, the affected user should immediately run SU53. The output shows the authorization object that failed, the field names checked, the values that were required by the AUTHORITY-CHECK statement, and the values actually present in the user's profile. This information is given directly to the basis team who uses PFCG to add the missing object with the required values to the user's role, then transports the role change to production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Support call: 'Report mein error aa raha hai, authorization something.' User ne SU53 run kiya — screenshot bheja. M_MSEG_WMB object, WERKS = 1100 missing tha. Basis team ne PFCG role mein 1100 add kiya — 15 minute resolution. SU53 ne investigation time 90% reduce kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SU53 run karna authorization error ke kaafi baad — SU53 sirf LAST failed check dikhata hai. Agar user ne kuch aur try kiya beech mein toh pichla authorization check overwrite ho jaata hai. Immediately SU53 run karo authorization error ke baad.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" SU53 Usage Guide (not ABAP code - process documentation):
" Step 1: User gets authorization error in selection screen
" Step 2: User runs transaction SU53 immediately
"         (or developer runs: SU53 -> Display for user: USERID)
" Step 3: SU53 shows:
"   Authorization Object: F_BKPF_BUK
"   Fields checked:  BUKRS = '2000', ACTVT = '03'
"   User profile has: BUKRS = '1000', ACTVT = '03'
"   Missing value: BUKRS '2000'

" Step 4: BASIS team -> PFCG -> User's Role -> Authorization
"   Find object F_BKPF_BUK -> Add BUKRS = '2000'
" Step 5: Save, Generate, Transport role to Production
" Step 6: User re-tests -> Authorization check passes

" In ABAP, provide helpful message directing to SU53:
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'Authorization failed. Run SU53 for details.' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: ACTVT (activity code) selection screen authorization mein kaise choose karte hain?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ACTVT authorization objects mein activity type represent karne wala ek standard SAP field hai. Standard values hain: 01 Create, 02 Change, 03 Display, 06 Delete, 08 Print, 16 Execute. Sirf data read aur display karne wale ek ABAP report ke liye, AUTHORITY-CHECK ko ACTVT = '03' use karna chahiye. Data update karne wale report ke liye, '02' use karo. Program execution authorization ke liye, '16' use hota hai. Ek bahut common mistake display report mein ACTVT = '01' use karna hai — yeh sirf display authorization (03) rakhne wale users ko report run karne se block karta hai, jabki create authorization (01) wale users ko allow karta hai jo intended audience bhi nahi ho sakte.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ACTVT is a standard SAP field in authorization objects representing the activity type. The standard values are: 01 Create, 02 Change, 03 Display, 06 Delete, 08 Print, 16 Execute. For an ABAP report that only reads and displays data, AUTHORITY-CHECK should use ACTVT = '03'. For a report that updates data, use '02'. For program execution authorization, '16' is used. A very common mistake is using ACTVT = '01' in a display report — this blocks users who have only display authorization (03) from running the report, while allowing users with create authorization (01) who may not even be the intended audience.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Developer ne report banaya sales analysis ke liye — sirf display. AUTHORITY-CHECK mein V_VBAK_AAT object, ACTVT = '01' likha. Sales team ke paas 03 tha — sab fail. Management (create authorization wale) run kar pa rahe the. Bug clearly: wrong ACTVT. Fix: ACTVT = '03'. Sab fixed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: ACTVT = '*' likhna — yeh any activity match karta hai — authorization bypass effectively. Hamesha specific ACTVT value use karo jo report ke actual function se match kare.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " WRONG: ACTVT 01 on a display-only report
  " AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'V_VBAK_AAT'
  "   ID 'AUART' FIELD p_auart
  "   ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'.  " Create - wrong for display!

  " CORRECT: ACTVT 03 for display report
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'V_VBAK_AAT'
    ID 'AUART' FIELD p_auart
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.  " Display
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |No display authorization for order type | &
            |{ p_auart }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

" ACTVT reference:
" 01 = Create,  02 = Change,  03 = Display
" 06 = Delete,  08 = Print,   16 = Execute
" Use the value matching the report's ACTUAL function

followupAnswerBank.question: Authorization issue kaise prevent karte hain development phase mein hi?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Production mein authorization issues development mein early security design incorporate karke prevent kiye jaate hain. Coding se pehle saare required objects aur activity values listing karne wala ek authorization concept document create karo. Development system mein, production business user profiles ko mirror karne wali ek minimal role ke saath ek restricted test user create karo. Is restricted user ke saath saare selection screen scenarios test karo, sirf developer ke full-authorization account ke saath nahi. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein AUTHORITY-CHECK ko ek standard step ke roop mein include karo, ek afterthought ke roop mein nahi. Ek restricted batch user ke saath background execution test karo. Transport se pehle ek security-focused peer ko AUTHORITY-CHECK coverage ke liye code review karwao.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization issues in production are prevented by incorporating security design early in development. Create an authorization concept document listing all required objects and activity values before coding. In the development system, create a restricted test user with a minimal role mirroring production business user profiles. Test all selection screen scenarios with this restricted user, not just with the developer's full-authorization account. Include AUTHORITY-CHECK in AT SELECTION-SCREEN as a standard step, not an afterthought. Test background execution with a restricted batch user. Have a security-focused peer review the code for AUTHORITY-CHECK coverage before transport.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project mein authorization concept document phela din hi banaya — object list, ACTVT values, user profiles. Dev mein 'TEST_RESTRICTED' user banaya with only display authorizations. Sab development testing us user se. Result: Zero authorization bugs in production. First time in that project's history.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization testing ko UAT tak postpone karna — UAT late stage pe authorization holes milti hain jab code freeze hota hai. Early testing: developer khud restricted user banake test kare — sirf 2 minutes extra effort, hours of production support saved.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Authorization-complete selection screen template:
REPORT zauth_complete_template.

" Step 1: Parameters with authorization-relevant fields
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

" Step 2: Pre-fill based on user's authorized scope (INITIALIZATION)
INITIALIZATION.
  " Optional: auto-fill user's primary company code
  " (from USR05 or Z-config table)

" Step 3: Authorization check immediately after user input
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.  " Always match to report function
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |Unauthorized: { p_bukrs }. SU53 for details.| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

" Step 4: Defense in depth - also check in data access
START-OF-SELECTION.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  CHECK sy-subrc = 0.  " Silent check - screen already validated

followupAnswerBank.question: Organizational level authorization (Org level) aur field-level authorization mein kya difference hai selection screen context mein?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAP authorization mein, organizational level fields — company code, plant, sales organization, purchasing organization — SU24 mein authorization object definition mein org-level marked hote hain. Yeh fields user roles mein values ke roop mein maintained hote hain aur broadest data access boundaries control karte hain. Field-level authorization fields — document type, material type, movement type — org-level scope ke andar finer-grained control provide karte hain. Selection screen pe, developer typically AUTHORITY-CHECK mein org-level fields check karta hai kyunki yeh primary data scope define karte hain jo user ko access karne ki permission hai. Highly sensitive sub-categories of data ke liye field-level checks add ho sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In SAP authorization, organizational level fields — company code, plant, sales organization, purchasing organization — are marked as org-level in the authorization object definition in SU24. These fields are maintained as values in user roles and control the broadest data access boundaries. Field-level authorization fields — document type, material type, movement type — provide finer-grained control within the org-level scope. On the selection screen, the developer typically checks org-level fields in AUTHORITY-CHECK because these define the primary data scope the user is allowed to access. Field-level checks may be added for highly sensitive sub-categories of data.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: MM report mein AUTHORITY-CHECK tha: M_MSEG_BWA object — BWART (movement type) = '101', ACTVT = '03'. Org level: WERKS (plant) = P1000. Both checked. User jo plant P1000 ka tha aur goods receipt (101) dekhne ka authorization tha woh run kar sakta tha. Different plant ya different BWART = fail. Clean layered authorization.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Org level check karna aur field level check miss karna — user apni company code mein sab document types dekh sakta hai jab sirf specific document types allow hone chahiye. Both levels check karo agar object includes both.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Org-level check: Company code (primary scope)
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs    " Org-level field
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE |No auth for company { p_bukrs }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Field-level check: Document type (sub-category)
  IF p_blart IS NOT INITIAL.
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BLA'
      ID 'BLART' FIELD p_blart  " Field-level restriction
      ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE |No auth for document type { p_blart }| TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
